steps.title: Identify the key signal about Dido's emotional state
steps.content:
The opening line—“anxious cares already seiz'd the queen”—signals that something significant has deeply affected Dido.

The passage then explains what: Aeneas's story, his character (valor, acts, and birth), and his appearance (words, his looks) have stirred her emotionally, imprinting themselves on her heart and intensifying her emotional suffering.

steps.title: Match this emotional impact to the passage's purpose
steps.content:
B directly states it: Dido has been deeply affected by Aeneas's story, as demonstrated through the anxious cares seizing her, the hidden flame burning in her veins, his character inspiring love in her soul, and the imprinting of his words and looks in her heart that increase her emotional pain.
  • A invents skepticism not present in the passage
  • C overstates by adding intellectual engagement the text doesn't develop
  • D misinterprets her “secret fire” as something hidden from Aeneas rather than her own hidden passion for him
.

This is the correct answer.
